ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-10-2025 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-06-2024 Continued To Feb-04-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO VACATE ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND/OR DEFAULT JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 473(B)
Off Calendar Oct-24-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. ELIZABETH SEARS, Esq. (tel: 510-717-1512) for Plaintiff.
Atty. IVAN HIGUERA, Esq. of Law Offices of Scott C Stratman (tel: 510-457-3440) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO VACATE ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND/OR DEFAULT JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 473(B).

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to vacate entry of default and default judgment is ordered off calendar as moot. No complaint is on file in this case. On November 8, 2023, the court denied plaintiff's motion to allow late-filed complaint. The denial was without prejudice to refiling the motion if plaintiff's counsel found case law to support her position. However, plaintiff now concedes: "No case law was found and the motion was never refiled." (Opp. 2:8-10.)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Oct-24-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. ELIZABETH SEARS, Esq. (tel: 510-717-1512) for Plaintiff.
Atty. IVAN HIGUERA, Esq. of Law Offices of Scott C Stratman (tel: 510-457-3440) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to quash service of summons is ordered off calendar as moot. No complaint is on file in this case. On November 8, 2023, the court denied plaintiff's motion to allow late-filed complaint. The denial was without prejudice to refiling the motion if plaintiff's counsel found case law to support her position. However, plaintiff now concedes: "No case law was found and the motion was never refiled." (Vac. Opp. 2:8-10.)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ELIZABETH C. SEARS, Esq. (Tel: 510-717-1512) for plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JEFFERSON CAMPOS' Motion To Vacate Entry Of Default And/Or Default Judgment Pursuant To C.C.P. 473(B).

Matter discussed.

The Court vacates its tentative ruling to continue this matter, and rules as follows: Matter is ordered off calendar. The parties are ordered to meet and confer on an agreed upon alternative continued hearing date for this matter to be heard on, and update the Court to have the matter placed back on calendar.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-06-2024 Continued To Apr-24-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Allow Late Filed Complaint To Be Treated As Timely Filed Under Ccp Sec 473(B)
Off Calendar Nov-08-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Elizabeth Sears, Esq. 510-717-1512 for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

Hearing is held. The Court shares concerns re statute of limitations. Ms. Sears responds. The Court informs Counsel she needs to refile and include a discussion on whether the statut of limitations is jurisdictional or not. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF KAVITA SHARMA's Motion To Allow Late Filed Complaint To Be Treated As Timely Filed Under CCP Sec 473(B).

Denied without Prejudice to refile.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)
